title: The compact-object mass gaps
statement: Two apparent gaps in the compact-object mass distribution are unexplained. The LOWER mass gap (~3-5 M_sun) lies between the heaviest neutron stars and the lightest black holes; X-ray binary data once suggested a real dearth there, but gravitational-wave events (e.g. GW190814's ~2.6 M_sun secondary, GW190425) populate or blur it. The UPPER (pair-instability) gap (~50-120 M_sun) is predicted because pair-instability supernovae leave no remnant; yet GW190521's ~85 M_sun component sits inside it. The open problem: are these gaps real features of stellar/supernova physics, or artifacts of selection and small samples — and what fills them (hierarchical mergers, accretion, exotic channels)?

originators.name: (community)
originators.note: lower gap from X-ray-binary mass measurements (Bailyn et al. 1998); upper gap from pair-instability supernova theory.

ranking.rationale: A stellar-astrophysics and population-statistics question (shallow fundamentally), being actively resolved by the growing gravitational-wave catalogue; the most tractable entry, placed at the bottom of the list.

key_ideas: Lower gap (~3-5 M_sun): X-ray binaries showed few objects there; whether it is a true gap depends on the supernova explosion mechanism (rapid vs delayed).
GW190814's secondary (~2.6 M_sun) is right in the lower gap — neutron star or black hole is unknown.

Upper (pair-instability) gap (~50-120 M_sun): electron-positron pair production in very massive stellar cores triggers an instability that leaves NO remnant, forbidding direct-collapse BHs in this range.
GW190521's ~85 M_sun component lies inside the upper gap, suggesting hierarchical (second-generation) mergers or uncertain pair-instability boundaries.
Selection effects and small samples can mimic or hide a gap; population inference is improving with each LIGO/Virgo/KAGRA run.
Resolving the gaps constrains supernova physics, the maximum neutron-star mass, and binary-evolution channels.

approaches.name: Gravitational-wave population inference
approaches.summary: Hierarchically model the merging-binary mass distribution to test for true gaps.

approaches.name: Supernova / stellar-evolution modeling
approaches.summary: Predict remnant masses (explosion mechanism, pair-instability) and the resulting gaps.

approaches.name: X-ray-binary mass measurements
approaches.summary: Dynamical masses of compact objects in X-ray binaries probing the lower gap.

papers.title: The Mass Distribution of Stellar Black Holes
papers.note: ApJ 499, 367. First Bayesian claim of a ~7 Msun clustering of BH masses in transient LMXBs and a significant gap above the neutron-star masses — the original empirical 'lower mass gap'.

papers.title: The Black Hole Mass Distribution in the Galaxy
papers.note: ApJ 725, 1918. 16 transient LMXBs give a narrow BH mass distribution at 7.8 +/- 1.2 Msun and argue the 2-5 Msun gap is not a selection effect — the canonical lower-gap reference.

papers.title: The Mass Distribution of Stellar-Mass Black Holes
papers.note: ApJ 741, 103. Bayesian model-selection over 20 X-ray binaries; finds M(1%) > 4.3-4.5 Msun, providing strong statistical evidence for the lower gap.

papers.title: Compact Remnant Mass Function: Dependence on the Explosion Mechanism and Metallicity
papers.note: ApJ 749, 91. The 'rapid' vs 'delayed' supernova-engine remnant-mass prescriptions used by essentially all population-synthesis codes; rapid engine produces a lower mass gap, delayed fills it.

papers.title: Missing Black Holes Unveil the Supernova Explosion Mechanism
papers.note: ApJ 757, 91. Argues the observed gap implies the core-collapse explosion is launched within ~100-200 ms by a rapidly growing instability; a fill-in would instead imply slower (>200 ms) growth.

papers.title: Mass Measurements of Black Holes in X-Ray Transients: Is There a Mass Gap?
papers.note: ApJ 757, 36. Systematic-error study of inclination/ellipsoidal modeling showing some gap candidates (e.g. GRO J0422+32) may move into the gap — a key caveat on the empirical gap.

papers.title: Very Massive Stars, Pair-Instability Supernovae and Intermediate-Mass Black Holes with the SEVN Code
papers.note: MNRAS 470, 4739. Implements PPISN/PISN in population synthesis, predicting an upper BH mass gap roughly 60-120 Msun — an early quantitative upper-gap modeling reference.

papers.title: A Noninteracting Low-Mass Black Hole-Giant Star Binary System
papers.note: Science 366, 637. 2MASS J05215658+4359220: a ~3.3 Msun unseen companion to a giant — a candidate non-interacting lower-gap object (its nature is debated; see van den Heuvel comment).

papers.title: Pulsational Pair-Instability Supernovae. I. Pre-collapse Evolution and Pulsational Mass Ejection
papers.note: ApJ 887, 72. Detailed hydrodynamic PPISN models setting the maximum BH mass below the upper gap near ~50 Msun, consistent with early LIGO/Virgo BH masses.

papers.title: The Impact of Pair-Instability Mass Loss on the Binary Black Hole Mass Distribution
papers.note: ApJ 882, 121. COMPAS isolated-binary models predicting a pile-up at 30-45 Msun and a cutoff near ~45 Msun from (P)PISN — an early link of the upper-gap edge to GW data.

papers.title: GW190814: Gravitational Waves from the Coalescence of a 23 Solar Mass Black Hole with a 2.6 Solar Mass Compact Object
papers.note: ApJL 896, L44. The 2.50-2.67 Msun secondary is either the lightest BH or heaviest NS observed; the flagship lower-gap GW event challenging compact-object formation models.

papers.title: Exploring the Lower Mass Gap and Unequal Mass Regime in Compact Binary Evolution
papers.note: ApJL 899, L1. Population modeling of GW190814-like systems; concludes that forming them requires a delayed/long-timescale engine, hinting the lower gap may be narrow or absent.

papers.title: A Lower Bound on the Maximum Mass if the Secondary in GW190814 Was Once a Rapidly Spinning Neutron Star
papers.note: MNRAS 499, L82. Uses universal relations to argue GW190814's secondary could be a (formerly) rapidly rotating NS, yielding M(TOV) > 2.08 Msun — links lower gap to the NS EoS.

papers.title: GW190521: A Binary Black Hole Merger with a Total Mass of 150 Solar Masses
papers.note: PRL 125, 101102. Primary ~85 Msun lies in the pair-instability (upper) mass gap with 99% probability; remnant ~142 Msun is an intermediate-mass black hole.

papers.title: Properties and Astrophysical Implications of the 150 Msun Binary Black Hole Merger GW190521
papers.note: ApJL 900, L13. Companion astrophysics paper discussing channels to populate the upper gap: stellar mergers, hierarchical mergers in clusters/AGN disks, and exotic alternatives.

papers.title: Constraints from Gravitational-Wave Detections of Binary Black Hole Mergers on the 12C(alpha,gamma)16O Rate
papers.note: ApJL 902, L36. Shows the lower edge of the upper gap is set mainly by the uncertain 12C(alpha,gamma)16O reaction rate; GW BH masses can in turn constrain that nuclear S-factor.

papers.title: Formation of GW190521 from Stellar Evolution: the Impact of the Hydrogen-Rich Envelope, Dredge-Up, and 12C(alpha,gamma)16O Rate on the Pair-Instability Black Hole Mass Gap
papers.note: MNRAS 501, 4514. Demonstrates the upper-gap lower edge can shift to ~70 Msun (or close entirely) given envelope collapse, dredge-up, and reaction-rate uncertainties — a model route to GW190521.

papers.title: The Impact of Stellar Rotation on the Black Hole Mass-Gap from Pair-Instability Supernovae
papers.note: A&A 640, L18. Shows the lower edge of the upper gap depends on rotation and angular-momentum transport (Spruit-Tayler dynamo), predicting a BH-spin dependence of the gap edge.

papers.title: Population Properties of Compact Objects from the Second LIGO-Virgo Gravitational-Wave Transient Catalog / Population of Merging Compact Binaries Inferred Using Gravitational Waves through GWTC-3
papers.note: PRX 13, 011048 (GWTC-3 population). Cannot yet confirm or rule out the lower gap; finds BH mass-spectrum features (peaks near chirp mass 8.3 and 27.9 Msun) and no sharp cutoff above ~60 Msun.

papers.title: Observation of Gravitational Waves from Two Neutron Star-Black Hole Coalescences
papers.note: ApJL 915, L5. GW200105 and GW200115, the first confident NS-BH mergers; their BH primaries (~5.7-8.9 Msun) and NS secondaries probe the edges of the lower gap.

papers.title: Bridging the Gap: Categorizing Gravitational-Wave Events at the Transition between Neutron Stars and Black Holes
papers.note: ApJ 931, 108. Population analysis placing the (tentative) lower-gap edges at ~2.2 and ~6.0 Msun and self-consistently classifying GW190814, GW200105, GW200115 at the NS-BH transition.

papers.title: Hierarchical Mergers of Stellar-Mass Black Holes and Their Gravitational-Wave Signatures
papers.note: Nature Astronomy 5, 749. Review of hierarchical (second+ generation) BH mergers as a way to populate the pair-instability gap, with the spin~0.7 signature and escape-speed requirements.

papers.title: The Pair-Instability Mass Gap for Black Holes
papers.note: ApJL 912, L31. Examines four factors (reaction rates, binary stripping, rotation, super-Eddington accretion) that can move the upper-gap boundaries, allowing M_low up to ~64-70 Msun.

papers.title: Observation of Gravitational Waves from the Coalescence of a 2.5-4.5 Msun Compact Object and a Neutron Star (GW230529)
papers.note: ApJL 970, L34. GW230529: a 2.5-4.5 Msun primary most plausibly a lower-mass-gap black hole merging with a neutron star — strongest GW evidence yet for objects inside the lower gap.

physicists.name: Charles D. Bailyn
physicists.contribution: Led the 1997 dynamical-mass study of transient LMXBs that first identified the empirical lower mass gap between neutron stars and black holes.

physicists.name: Feryal Ozel
physicists.affiliation: Georgia Institute of Technology (formerly University of Arizona)
physicists.contribution: Authored the 2010 Galactic BH mass-distribution paper establishing the narrow ~7.8 Msun BH peak and arguing the 2-5 Msun gap is physical, not a selection effect.

physicists.name: Vicky Kalogera
physicists.contribution: Co-developed compact-binary population synthesis and the statistical/theoretical framing of the mass gap and the supernova-engine remnant-mass prescriptions.

physicists.name: Chris L. Fryer
physicists.contribution: Built the rapid- vs delayed-explosion remnant-mass formulae (Fryer et al. 2012) that determine whether population-synthesis models produce or fill the lower mass gap.

physicists.name: Krzysztof Belczynski
physicists.contribution: Argued (2012) that the 'missing' low-mass black holes constrain the timescale of the core-collapse supernova explosion mechanism.

physicists.name: Stan E. Woosley
physicists.contribution: Pioneered the stellar-evolution theory of pair-instability and pulsational-pair-instability supernovae that creates the upper (pair-instability) black-hole mass gap.

physicists.name: Alexander Heger
physicists.contribution: Co-developed massive-star and pair-instability supernova models defining the location and uncertainties of the ~50-130 Msun upper mass gap.

physicists.name: Maya Fishbach
physicists.contribution: Leads gravitational-wave population inference probing both gaps, including hierarchical-merger signatures and categorizing events at the NS-BH transition.

physicists.name: Michela Mapelli
physicists.contribution: Developed the SEVN population-synthesis treatment of PPISN/PISN and dynamical formation channels that populate the upper mass gap.

physicists.name: Robert Farmer
physicists.affiliation: Max Planck Institute for Astrophysics / University of Amsterdam
physicists.contribution: Showed the upper-gap lower edge is governed by the 12C(alpha,gamma)16O reaction rate, tying gravitational-wave BH masses to nuclear astrophysics.

simulator_angle: Mass-agnostic for the GR renderer; relevant as the population context (which masses of Kerr black holes actually exist) rather than a distinct geometry.

metadata.coverage_note: Assembled 24 verified, genuinely-relevant papers and 10 physicists for the compact-object mass-gap problem. Method: ran 13 varied Consensus MCP searches (mcp__plugin_bio-research_consensus__search) across the lower mass gap (NS-BH), upper/pair-instability mass gap, GW190814, GW190521, GW230529, NS-BH mergers, X-ray-binary and Gaia dynamical mass measurements, supernova-engine remnant-mass theory, PPISN/PISN stellar modeling, hierarchical mergers, the 12C(alpha,gamma)16O reaction-rate edge, and GWTC-3/GWTC-4 population inference. Every paper carries a real Consensus/arXiv-backed identifier.url returned by the search tool. No invented entries; none of the famous-classic-by-memory slots were needed since search surfaced the foundational landmarks (Bailyn 1997, Ozel 2010, Farr 2010, Fryer 2011, Belczynski 2012) directly. This is an OPEN problem: the existence/width of both gaps is still unresolved, so observational papers are tagged observational_constraint, population-inference and stellar-modeling resolution claims are open_conjecture, and the LVK detection papers are observational_constraint. Counts: 24 papers, 10 physicists.
